feedback: POSITIVE: Permission check in cancelRequest correctly validates requested_by === user?.uid (line 459 in useAssignmentRequests.tsx)
POSITIVE: Cancel button properly filtered to only show for pending transfers (line 217 in TransfersList.tsx: showCancel={r.status === 'pending'})
POSITIVE: Strong unit tests exist for approveRequest and rejectRequest batch operations in useAssignmentRequests.test.tsx
POSITIVE: Comprehensive E2E tests cover cancel workflow in transfers.spec.ts ([XFER-OUTGOING-2] through [XFER-OUTGOING-5])
POSITIVE: RTL compatibility maintained with dir attribute properly passed to cancel button and dialog (lines 151, 263, 281)
GAP: No unit test specifically validates the permission check in cancelRequest - only the requester should be able to cancel
GAP: Firestore security rules only allow update for admins/leaders (line 283 in firestore.rules) but don't have a specific rule for users canceling their own requests
GAP: No unit test verifies that cancelRequest throws error when user?.uid does not match requested_by
IMPROVEMENT: E2E test [XFER-OUTGOING-4] validates UI-level permission (only sender sees cancel button) but backend permission enforcement needs unit test coverage
IMPROVEMENT: Consider adding error handling test cases for edge cases like missing user context or already-processed requests
nextSteps: Add unit test in useAssignmentRequests.test.tsx to verify cancelRequest throws error when requested_by !== user?.uid
Add unit test to verify cancelRequest succeeds when requested_by === user?.uid
Update Firestore security rules to explicitly allow users to update their own pending requests to rejected status (self-cancellation)
Add integration test to verify Firestore rules correctly reject cancel attempts from non-requesters
Consider adding error toast message test to verify proper user feedback when cancel fails
Add test for edge case where user context is null during cancel attempt
